This report presents investigative leads based on transaction pattern analysis. It does not establish guilt — all findings should be independently verified before action.

Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 2,453,232.74 was moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 1,002,498.15 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • The largest source of funds was account 2653101012274 belonging to DEEPA CHAUHAN, which sent out Rs 469,133.00.
  • There was 1 intermediary account that received and then passed on funds.
  • DEEPA CHAUHAN's account 2653101012274 sent money to 289 different accounts.
  • DEEPA CHAUHAN's account 2653101012274 received money from 111 different accounts.
  • Rs 5,649,803.56 of the incoming funds was used for transfers or withdrawals, but Rs 152.44 of the funds could not be fully traced.
  • The first account to investigate is YASH DUBEY's account 25078124219247, which shows the most suspicious behaviors in this case.

Account Number 25078124219247 · DCB Bank · Score 2,930
Rank #1

The account YASH DUBEY (25078124219247) exhibits suspicious money behavior, with a large credit of 400000.00 traced into 17 subsequent debits, and multiple patterns indicating potential money laundering or pass-through activity. The account's high score of 2930 and strong patterns of 29 suggest a high risk of illicit activity. The presence of round-value debits and a high throughput ratio further supports this suspicion.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 400000.00 was traced into 17 subsequent debits, with 400000.00 allocated and 0.00 remaining untraced, indicating potential money laundering. (+28 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions ref:681310110010629 are duplicates, sharing an account, direction, near-equal amount, and nearby date, but were not placed in the duplicate bucket by extraction. (+2 more of this type)
  • Pass Through Routing Account weak — The account processed 82647786.36 in combined inflow/outflow with a 100.0% throughput ratio, consistent with pass-through use across multiple counterparties.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 665 nodes and 1239 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex network of transactions.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 9 suspicious round-value debits totalling 277000.00, with repeated amounts of 25,000.00, indicating potential structuring activity.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
